doc_title: California State Bar Emergency Access to Justice Protocol 2025 (EAJP-2025)
label: fabricated
reason: No verifiable "EAJP-2025" protocol exists from the State Bar of California, and its provisions (e.g., unsupervised paralegal filing, UPL waiver) conflict with established California law.
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: The State Bar of California has no publicly known "Emergency Access to Justice Protocol" or "EAJP-2025," the Legal Services Trust Fund Commission does not issue protocols authorizing paralegals to independently draft and file motions without attorney supervision (which would conflict with California's unauthorized-practice-of-law framework under Bus. & Prof. Code §6125), and details such as the "EAJP-Form 1A," the specific 72-hour waiver of attorney review, and the cited post-conviction relief provision (§1170(d), which was renumbered/amended) all appear invented.
response_turn2:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 92
REASON: No verifiable "EAJP-2025" protocol exists from the State Bar of California, and its provisions (e.g., unsupervised paralegal filing, UPL waiver) conflict with established California law.
